data.title: U.S. and Chinese officials set for trade talks in London on Monday
data.description:
U.S. President Donald Trump on Friday said three members of his administration would hold trade talks with Chinese officials in the United Kingdom on June 9. The announcement comes a day after he held a "very good phone call" with China's leader Xi Jinping.

"I am pleased to announce that Secretary of the Treasury Scott Bessent, Secretary of Commerce Howard Lutnick, and United States Trade Representative, Ambassador Jamieson Greer, will be meeting in London on Monday, June 9, 2025, with Representatives of China, with reference to the Trade Deal. The meeting should go very well," Trump said on his Truth Social service. 

Trump said his call with Xi on Thursday "resulted in a very positive conclusion" for the world's two largest economies, adding that there "should no longer be any questions respecting the complexity of Rare Earth products."

The President said he had been invited by Xi to visit China, and that he had also "reciprocated."

The upcoming meeting in London will be the second between U.S. and Chinese officials since discussions held in Switzerland in mid-May. Those talks had yielded in a surprise temporary trade truce.

But Trump last Friday reignited trade tensions between the countries after accusing China of violating that truce.           

Wall Street on Friday ended sharply higher after a strong May jobs report boosted sentiment and took away the spotlight from trade developments.    

Here are some exchange-traded funds that track the benchmark S&P 500 index (SP500): (NYSEARCA:SPY), (NYSEARCA:VOO), (NYSEARCA:IVV), (NYSEARCA:RSP), (NYSEARCA:SSO), (NYSEARCA:UPRO), (NYSEARCA:SH), (NYSEARCA:SDS), and (NYSEARCA:SPXU).

And here are some China-linked ETFs: (NYSEARCA:KWEB), (NASDAQ:PGJ), (NYSEARCA:CQQQ), (NYSEARCA:FXI), (NYSEARCA:GXC), (NASDAQ:MCHI), (NYSEARCA:FLCH), (BATS:CNYA), (NYSEARCA:ASHR), and (NYSEARCA:YINN).

Dear readers: We recognize that politics often intersects with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.

data.title: The Oncology Institute files for $50M common stock offering
data.description:
• The Oncology Institute (NASDAQ:TOI) has filed a shelf registration to offer and sell up to $50M of its common stock.
  • TOI shares rise 2% post-market on Friday.
  • Source: SEC filing

data.title: Deere gains as Melius upgrades, seen near recurring revenue upside from data offerings
data.description:
Deere (NYSE:DE) closed +1.8% on Friday as Melius Research upgraded shares to Buy from Neutral with a two-year $750 price target, saying the agricultural equipment maker is moving closer to realizing meaningful recurring revenue from its software and data offerings.

Deere (NYSE:DE) aims to generate ~10% of sales from recurring revenue by 2030, or ~$5B, and Melius analysts said the company's "full-stack" offering, integrating advanced hardware, sensors, software, and a strong dealer network, creates a formidable barrier for competitors, and these systems already are delivering measurable benefits for farmers and will become increasingly indispensable over time.

While tech investors have yet to fully engage with Deere (DE), Melius said the company is 5-10 years ahead in data capture and usage, aided by its Operations Center platform and a shift in farmer attitudes toward SaaS-like models.

"Investors can forecast with high confidence that Deere will lead in ag tech, whether in 5 years or 15+," Melius wrote.

data.title: Mingteng International files $100M mixed shelf offering
data.description:
• Mingteng International (NASDAQ:MTEN) has filed a $100M shelf registration to offer a variety of securities, including ordinary shares, warrants, debt, rights, and units.
  • Proceeds from any sale will be available for immediate use, though the company warns of risks related to effective deployment of capital.
  • Source: SEC filing

data.title: Opendoor Technologies seeks approval for reverse stock split
data.description:
• Opendoor Technologies (NASDAQ:OPEN) has filed a preliminary proxy statement seeking the board's approval for a reverse stock split, the company said on Friday.
  • The company may put into effect a reverse stock split at a ratio ranging from any whole number between 1‑for‑10 and 1‑for‑50, it added.
  • OPEN -5.26% after hours to $0.644.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Crypto exchange Gemini files confidentially for U.S. IPO
data.description:
Gemini, the crypto exchange and custodian founded by Cameron and Tyler Winklevoss, has confidentially filed for an initial public offering, according to a U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission filing submitted on Friday.

Details regarding the size and pricing of the offering were not disclosed in the draft registration statement.

The IPO is expected to move forward following the completion of the SEC's review process.

The preparation for a listing comes as U.S. regulatory agencies back off of Gemini and the crypto space in general. In February, the U.S. SEC closed an investigation into the exchange.

Earlier this week, stablecoin issuer Circle Internet Group (CRCL) debuted on the New York Stock Exchange, with the stock surging in its first two trading sessions.

data.title: S&P 500 closes above 6,000 for the first time since Feb after strong May jobs data
data.description:
Wall Street's major averages on Friday took home gains, with the S&P 500 reclaiming the 6,000 level for the first time since late February. Sentiment was boosted by a strong May jobs report.

The benchmark S&P 500 (SP500) added +1% to end at 6,000.36 points, its first close above that mark since February 21. Meanwhile, the tech-focused Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) ended +1.2%, and the Dow (DJI) finished +1.1%. 

Week-to-date, the S&P gained +1.5%, the Nasdaq Composite added +2.3%, and the Dow was up +1.2%.

Over in the bond market, U.S. Treasury yields rallied. The 10-year yield (US10Y) rose 12 basis points to 4.51%. The 2-year yield (US2Y) also rose 12 basis points to 4.05%.

Before the opening bell, May Nonfarm payrolls showed an increase of +139K vs. +126K consensus and +147K prior (revised from +177K), according to the U.S. Bureau of Labor Statistics.

Also, the unemployment rate came in unchanged at 4.2%, in line with consensus.

After the report, Trump once again blasted Federal Reserve chief Jerome Powell for not lowering interest rates. 

“Traders are cheering this morning’s better-than-expected Friday Jobs report and are picking up stocks hand over fist, sending the S&P 500 above the monumental 6,000 level," said José Torres, Senior Economist at Interactive Brokers.

"May’s payroll performance was the second strongest of the year and is dampening concerns related to trade conflicts while quelling fears of an economic slowdown," Torres added. "But despite President Trump’s calls for the Fed to cut immediately, rates are jumping in bear flattening fashion as buoyant growth expectations and a lack of safe-haven demand drive yields north. The risk-on posture is further evidenced by weakening gold prices as well as the unwinding of volatility protection instruments."

Wall Street closed in the red on Thursday as Tesla (TSLA) shaved off more than $100B in market capitalization amid a public verbal spat between U.S. President Donald Trump and CEO Elon Musk.

Turning to trade developments, Reuters reported that China had granted temporary export licenses for rare earth suppliers to the top three U.S. automakers, citing two sources familiar with the matter. China's strict export controls over rare earths has been a flashpoint in U.S. trade relations.  

Trump on Thursday said he had held a "very good phone call" with China's leader Xi Jinping which "resulted in a very positive conclusion" for the two nations.

data.title: MercadoLibre lowers free shipping minimum
data.description:
• Shares of MercadoLibre (NASDAQ:MELI) were under pressure on Friday after the e-commerce giant said it lowered the threshold for free shipping in Brazil to compete with rival Amazon (AMZN) in the Latin American market.
  • The company will now offer free shipping on orders of $3.40 or more from the prior minimum of $14.15.
  • According to Reuters, the head of the company’s e-commerce operations in Brazil said MercadoLibre will absorb the additional costs of shipping.
  • MercadoLibre (NASDAQ:MELI) holds a 35% market share in Brazil versus 16% held by Amazon (AMZN), which offers free shipping to on all purchases to Prime members.

data.title: BloomZ granted second 180-day extension to meet Nasdaq $1 bid price rule
data.description:
• BloomZ (NASDAQ:BLMZ) received a second 180-day extension from Nasdaq, until December 1, 2025, to meet the minimum $1 bid price requirement.
  • BLMZ was first notified of non-compliance on December 2, 2024, with an initial deadline of June 2, 2025.
  • BLMZ shares up 4.5% post-market on Friday.
  • Source: Press release

data.title: Arcos Dorados appoints Luis Raganato as CEO
data.description:
• Arcos Dorados (NYSE:ARCO), the world’s largest independent McDonald’s franchisee, has appointed Luis Raganato as its new chief executive officer, effective July 1, 2025, the company said on Friday.
  • Raganato succeeds Marcelo Rabach, who decided to step down from the CEO position but will remain a member of the company’s board of directors.
  • The company has also appointed Carlos Gonzalez as its new chief operating officer.
  • Source: Press Release
